headline: Tesla Education becomes fifth Ho Chi Minh City school with full IB Continuum
dek: The school completed four international accreditations within 12 months, culminating in IB Diploma Programme authorisation in April 2026.
author: marcus-chen

body.text: Tesla Education, a Ho Chi Minh City-based international school, has received authorisation for the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (DP), making it the fifth institution in the city to offer the full IB Continuum — spanning the Primary Years Programme (PYP), Middle Years Programme (MYP) and Diploma Programme — according to VnExpress International.

The DP authorisation, granted in April 2026, followed three other accreditations obtained over the preceding ten months: Council of International Schools (CIS) accreditation in June 2025, Cambridge accreditation in July 2025, and IB MYP authorisation in November 2025. The school said the pace of credentialing reflected a five-year development strategy rather than an accelerated push.

Each IB authorisation requires schools to satisfy hundreds of standards set by the International Baccalaureate Organization (IBO), covering educational philosophy, teacher qualifications, governance, student support and infrastructure. Schools that complete the full Continuum allow students to progress through a single pedagogical framework from primary through to pre-university level, avoiding what the IBO describes as a 'methodological shock' for students who enter the IB system only at the diploma stage.

Alongside its IB pathway, Tesla Education maintains the Cambridge curriculum and its CIS accreditation. The school describes this combination as a multi-layered international education model intended to serve both expatriate and locally enrolled families seeking internationally recognised qualifications.

IB authorisation is widely regarded in Vietnam's international education sector as a marker of academic quality, and the number of fully authorised Continuum schools in Ho Chi Minh City remains small relative to the city's broader international school market. The addition of a fifth provider may increase competitive pressure on established IB schools in the city, particularly as demand for full-pathway programmes grows among families planning university applications abroad.

Tesla Education did not release enrolment figures or fee schedules in connection with the announcement. The school is separately accredited by Cambridge Assessment International Education, whose qualifications are commonly used alongside or as an alternative to the IB at international schools across Southeast Asia.
